title: Move movie scrub targets in 15-second steps inside the original scrub session
summary: 0.6.57 keeps non-trigger movie joystick inside the original paused scrub-session choreography but moves its target by one discrete 15-second step per stick excursion, so joystick video distance improves without the repeated live skip seeks that disturbed sync in 0.6.56.
changes: Non-trigger movie joystick input now advances the paused scrub-session target by one discrete 15-second step per stick excursion.
The scrub session still pauses once, accumulates target changes while held, seeks once on release, and resumes once.
Image stepping and trigger-modified video next and previous behavior remain unchanged.
reasoning: 0.6.56 proved that repeated live skip seeks gave the desired distance but disturbed sync because they changed the seek cadence and playback-state choreography. This slice keeps the original scrub-session model and changes it to one 15-second target step per stick excursion inside the same controlled finalize-on-release path.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: Each non-trigger movie joystick nudge should produce one 15-second transport step again.
Audio and video sync should stay closer because the gesture returns to one paused scrub session with one finalize seek.
Image stepping stays smooth and incremental.
knownIssues: This slice does not change slideshow, right-stick volume, or deferred gaze work.
This slice does not change trigger-modified video next and previous behavior.
verification: The non-trigger movie joystick branch in FASyncRuntime.CuaPlayerInputModule.cs keeps BeginCuaPlayerVideoScrubSession and EndCuaPlayerVideoScrubSession while moving the target in StandalonePlayerDefaultSkipSeconds-sized steps.
No repeated Player.SkipForward or Player.SkipBackward calls remain in the non-trigger movie joystick branch.
